data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: WhatsApp uses a microservices architecture to handle various functionalities separately, improving scalability and maintainability.
data.technical_spec: Services are containerized and orchestrated using Docker and Kubernetes.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Messages are end-to-end encrypted, ensuring only communicating users can read them.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes the Signal Protocol for encryption.

data.title: Real-Time Communication
data.description: Utilizes WebRTC for real-time communication, enabling voice and video calls.
data.technical_spec: Peer-to-peer connection with STUN and TURN servers support.

data.title: Data Replication
data.description: Data is replicated across multiple data centers for redundancy and low latency.
data.technical_spec: Cassandra is used for distributed data management.

data.title: Push Notification System
data.description: WhatsApp relies on push notifications to alert users of new messages.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes APNs for iOS and FCM for Android devices.
